title: TNLA/PSLF Issues Statement Welcoming Min Aung Hlaing's Government — Major Three Brotherhood Alliance Fracture
detail: On April 15, the Palaung State Liberation Front / Ta'ang National Liberation Army (PSLF/TNLA) Central Executive Committee issued a formal statement welcoming the new government formed by Min Aung Hlaing following his April 10 presidential inauguration. The statement represents an extraordinary political reversal for TNLA — the organization that co-founded the Three Brotherhood Alliance and co-launched Operation 1027 in October 2023 alongside MNDAA and the Arakan Army. TNLA's shift toward political accommodation is widely attributed to sustained Chinese diplomatic pressure, as China has consistently sought to prevent resistance forces from achieving a complete military victory that could destabilize the border region. China has particularly pressed TNLA and MNDAA (historically China-aligned EAOs) to disengage from full-scale military operations and return to ceasefire arrangements. The TNLA statement breaks explicitly from the NUG-led resistance coalition's position that Min Aung Hlaing's inauguration has no legitimacy. MNDAA's position has not been publicly clarified as of April 15. The fracture raises questions about whether the Three Brotherhood Alliance can sustain coordinated military operations, and whether TNLA's territorial gains in northern Shan State will be consolidated or negotiated away in subsequent talks.

media.caption: PSLF/TNLA welcomed the government formed by Min Aung Hlaing — a potential major fracture in the Three Brotherhood Alliance

title: Steering Council for Federal Democratic Union (SCEF) — NUG, KIO, KNU, KNPP, CNF Form Unified Resistance Command
detail: A new umbrella resistance structure, the Steering Council for the Emergence of a Federal Democratic Union (SCEF), was formally announced and analyzed in mid-April 2026 following its establishment on approximately March 30. The SCEF consolidates the National Unity Government (NUG), Committee Representing Pyidaungsu Hluttaw (CRPH), Kachin Independence Organization (KIO), Karen National Union (KNU), Karenni National Progressive Party (KNPP), and Chin National Front (CNF) under a joint political and military coordinating structure. Security analysts at the Foundation for Defense of Democracies described the SCEF as a historically significant step toward unified governance for resistance-controlled territories — an unprecedented degree of institutional coordination among Myanmar's diverse anti-junta forces. The formation of the SCEF comes as the TNLA simultaneously signals political accommodation with the junta, creating a bifurcated picture: some EAOs deepening integration with the NUG-led federal framework, while others pursue separate negotiations under Chinese influence. The KNU, KIA, KNPP, and CNF joining the SCEF represents the core of the ethnic-democratic coalition that fought the junta without ceasefire engagement since 2021. Analysis suggests the SCEF's governing charter provides a framework for transitional administration of liberated areas ahead of any eventual post-conflict settlement.

media.caption: Myanmar's NUG and four major ethnic armies form unified resistance command structure under SCEF
